content:
**Real Madrid Clinch Record 37th La Liga Title After Dramatic Final Day**

*Madrid, May 25, 2025* — Real Madrid have been crowned La Liga champions for the 2024/25 season, securing their record-extending 37th league title after a thrilling 2-1 victory over Villarreal at the Santiago Bernabéu on Sunday.

Heading into the final matchday, Los Blancos needed just a point to stay ahead of rivals Barcelona, who finished their campaign with a 3-0 win over Sevilla. But Carlo Ancelotti’s men left nothing to chance, with goals from Jude Bellingham and Vinícius Júnior ensuring the title returned to the Spanish capital.

Club captain Nacho Fernández lifted the trophy amid jubilant scenes, telling reporters, “This group has shown incredible determination all season. The support from our fans has been unbelievable—we did this for them.”

Real Madrid’s consistency throughout the campaign proved decisive, with standout performances from youngsters Arda Güler and Eduardo Camavinga complementing the experience of veterans like Toni Kroos, who announced his retirement after the match.

Barcelona, under Xavi Hernández, pushed their rivals all the way but ultimately fell short by two points. Atlético Madrid rounded out the top three, securing Champions League qualification.

Ancelotti dedicated the triumph to the club’s supporters: “Every title is special, but this one, with so many young talents stepping up, feels even more satisfying.”

Real Madrid now turn their focus to a potential double, as they prepare for next week’s Copa del Rey final.
